index.name: Nifty Metal Index
index.description: Nifty Metal tracks 15 companies spanning steel (Tata Steel 18.5%, JSW Steel 15.7%, SAIL 7.6%), aluminium (Hindalco 17.2% through Novelis), zinc (Hindustan Zinc 8.9% within Vedanta 10.3%), and diversified miners (Vedanta). Highly cyclical with commodity price correlation — when global growth accelerates, construction/manufacturing demand surge; metals rally. Chinese demand drives 40%+ of global commodity prices, thus India metals benefit from China's stimulus cycles. Structural tailwind: India's infrastructure capex targeting ₹200 Lakh Cr through 2030 (roads, rail, ports) requires 10x current steel production. Steel sector capacity utter utilization currently 70%, suggesting 30-50% margin for organic growth without capex. Novelis (Hindalco subsidiary) generates 60%+ value — aluminium foil for beverage cans in structural deficit (demand growing 8% annually as can penetration in India rises). Currency headwind: when rupee weakens, imported scrap iron/coal costs rise, compressing margins. Margin expansion cycle: when ore prices collapse (e.g., due to China oversupply), revenue falls but EBITDA margins expand from 25% to 35% if fixed costs absorb. Debt concerns: sector historically financed through leverage (debt-to-EBITDA 3-4x); rising rates constrain expansion.

holdings.name: Tata Steel
holdings.description: India's largest integrated steel company. Tata Steel Europe (Corus) acquisition expanded global footprint. JhariaCoal mines for captive use. Beneficiary of infrastructure boom and auto sector demand.

holdings.name: Hindalco Industries
holdings.description: World's largest aluminium rolling company via Novelis (USA/Canada). Domestic copper smelting via Birlacopper. Novelis contributes 60%+ revenues and is the key re-rating driver.

holdings.name: JSW Steel
holdings.description: India's largest steel producer by capacity (27 MTPA target). Coastal plants enable imported coal advantage. Strong automotive and real estate demand. JSW One digital procurement platform.

holdings.name: Vedanta Ltd
holdings.description: Multi-metal conglomerate: zinc (Hindustan Zinc), aluminium, iron ore, copper, oil & gas. High dividend policy. Zinc business is world-class asset.

holdings.name: Hindustan Zinc
holdings.description: World's second-largest zinc-lead miner and producer. Rajpura Dariba and Sindesar Khurd mines. Rampura Agucha is one of the world's largest open-pit zinc mines. 65% owned by Vedanta.

holdings.name: SAIL (Steel Authority)
holdings.description: India's largest PSU steel company. Integrated plants at Bhilai, Rourkela, Bokaro, Durgapur, Burnpur. Government infra orders provide stable demand. Target 50 MTPA capacity by 2030.

holdings.name: APL Apollo Tubes
holdings.description: India's largest structural steel tubes manufacturer. Branded products command premium pricing. Building materials and infrastructure construction applications. Asset-light distribution model.

holdings.name: National Aluminium
holdings.description: India's only integrated bauxite-alumina-aluminium PSU. Captive power plants and Damanjodi alumina refinery. Low-cost producer with long-term export contracts.

holdings.name: Ratnamani Metals
holdings.description: Premium stainless steel and carbon steel pipes for oil & gas, fertilizers, power. High-margin specialty products. Strong order book from refinery and petrochemical capex.

holdings.name: Hindustan Copper
holdings.description: India's only vertically integrated copper producer. Khetri and Malanjkhand mines. Benefits from global copper demand surge driven by electric vehicles and renewable energy transition.

name: Mirae Asset Mutual Fund - Mirae Asset Nifty Metal ETF

index.name: Nifty Metal Total Return Index
index.description: Mirae Asset Mutual Fund - Mirae Asset Nifty Metal ETF tracks the Nifty Metal Total Return Index index.

holdings.name: Tata Steel
holdings.description: India's largest steel company by revenue with UK (Corus/Port Talbot) and Netherlands operations. 20 MT India capacity. Auto-grade, galvanised and construction-grade steel leader. Net-zero steel by 2045 target via EAF and green hydrogen.

holdings.name: Hindalco Industries
holdings.description: Aditya Birla Group's metals flagship. Birla Copper (India's largest copper smelter) + Novelis (world's largest aluminium recycler, via NY). Novelis recycles used beverage cans for Ford, BMW — circular economy model. Aluminium demand driven by EV battery casings, aerospace.

holdings.name: JSW Steel
holdings.description: India's most efficient steel producer. Vijayanagar (Karnataka) plant among world's lowest cost. 27 MT India capacity; Dolvi expansion complete. US JV (JSW Steel USA) diversifies geography. Downstream value-add (colour-coated, pipes) margin expansion.

holdings.name: Coal India
holdings.description: World's largest coal mining company. 650+ MT annual production; 80%+ of India's thermal coal supply. Essential for base-load power generation while India transitions to renewables. High dividend yield (6-8%) from profitable PSU cash generation.

holdings.name: Vedanta
holdings.description: India's largest zinc producer (Hindustan Zinc sub — world's 2nd largest) + aluminium (BALCO, MALCO) + oil & gas (Cairn) + iron ore (Goa). Vedanta Resources (parent) delisting attempt; ongoing promoter restructuring. High FCF, high dividend payout history.

holdings.name: NMDC
holdings.description: India's largest iron ore producer (35+ MT annually). Chhattisgarh and Karnataka mines feed India's entire steelmaking demand. New Nagarnar integrated steel plant (3 MT) adds downstream value. Export optionality when domestic prices exceed seaborne parity.

name: Groww Mutual Fund - Groww Nifty Metal ETF
